reasoning: The current value of the RRPONTTLD series for 2026-03-29 (based on the last business day, Friday 2026-03-27) is 0.992 billion. By April 5 (which resolves to Friday April 3), the value is highly unlikely to be higher than 0.992. Over the last three weeks, daily RRP usage has fluctuated between 0.137 and 1.123, with a mean around 0.66 and strong mean-reversion. The elevated 0.992 value on March 27 exhibits pre-quarter-end window dressing behavior as market participants adjust balance sheets ahead of March 31. By early April (April 3), this quarter-end distortion will have passed, and RRP balances typically revert to their lower baseline (likely in the 0.400 - 0.700 billion range). Furthermore, the structural drain on total liquidity heading into April tax season adds secondary downward pressure. I assign a low probability (22%) that random variance will push a normal post-quarter-end day above the somewhat elevated 0.992 threshold.
